inline_comments.message:
In the old packer we used to have:
apt-get -f install -y
to fix dependencies after installing Erlang. 
Will this line take care of that?
no idea. what's apt-get install -y without any packages do?

apt-get -f install -y with no packages seems like it fixes dependencies by selecting a solution.. but if a package is specified looks like the package needs to be the solution for the dependency. 

In the old packer looks like we might have been having dependency issues since Erlang was installed like this:

    wget https://packages.erlang-solutions.com/erlang/esl-erlang/FLAVOUR_1_general/esl-erlang_19.3.6-1~ubuntu~trusty_amd64.deb
    dpkg -i esl-erlang_19.3.6-1~ubuntu~trusty_amd64.deb
    apt-get install -y libwxbase3.0-0v5
    apt-get -f install -y

I want to test this, but i still cannot get past my issues with ansible after getting my network issues magically fixed on Friday.
My guess is that install_recommends will take care of any Erlang dependencies..
